2852	\section{The invertibility-carrier Picard group}
2853	\label{sec:tensorobj_pic_carrier}
2854	
2855	The group law of \cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup} is carried on the
2856	\emph{locally-trivial} predicate \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\), and on that carrier
2857	the existence of a tensor inverse for each class is an extra obligation
2858	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}): one must \emph{manufacture} the dual
2859	object \(L^{-1} = \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)\) and prove the
2860	contraction \(L \otimes_X L^{-1} \cong \mathcal{O}_X\), which is the whole content of
2861	the dual-infrastructure build of \cref{sec:tensorobj_dual_infra}.
2862	
2863	This section carries the group law on a \emph{different} predicate for which that
2864	obligation evaporates: the \(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate
2865	\(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible},
2866	\[
2867	  \mathtt{IsInvertible}(M)
2868	  \;:\equiv\;
2869	  \exists\, N,\ \mathrm{Nonempty}\bigl(M \otimes_X N \cong \mathcal{O}_X\bigr).
2870	\]
2871	On this carrier the inverse of a class \([M]\) is supplied by the \emph{membership
2872	witness itself} --- the very \(N\) whose existence \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}(M)\)
2873	asserts --- so no inverse object need be constructed and no separate
2874	tensor-inverse lemma is consumed. The cost is shifted to a routine
2875	well-definedness fact (\cref{lem:isinvertible_inverse_welldef}: the witness \(N\) is
2876	determined up to isomorphism), proved from the monoidal coherence isomorphisms
2877	alone. The carrier is the scheme-theoretic analogue of Mathlib's
2878	\(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\), whose elements are exactly the iso-classes of invertible
2879	modules (Stacks tag 01CX).

878	whiskering the sheafification unit `η` (a `J.W`-morphism, hence locally bijective)
879	on each side and inverting under `a` via `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` together
880	with the flatness-free `W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W`. It is the bridge reconciling a
881	presheaf-level tensorator with the substrate `⊗_X` (whose `.val` carries an extra
882	sheafification on each factor), as needed by the pullback-monoidality comparison
883	`pullbackTensorIso`. -/
884	private noncomputable def sheafifyTensorUnitIso {X : Scheme.{u}}
885	    (P Q : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
886	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
887	        (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) P Q) ≅
888	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
889	        (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf)
890	          ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification
891	              (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj P).val
892	          ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification
893	              (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj Q).val) := by
894	  letI instMS : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf)) :=
895	    inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategoryStruct
896	      (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
897	  set a := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) with ha
898	  set η := (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
899	    (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit with hη
900	  have hηP : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
901	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app P)) := by
902	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
903	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
904	  have hηQ : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
905	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app Q)) := by
906	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
907	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
908	  have hW1 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
909	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app P ▷ Q)) :=
910	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W (R := X.presheaf) Q (η.app P) hηP
911	  have hW2 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
912	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map ((a.obj P).val ◁ η.app Q)) :=
913	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_W (R := X.presheaf) (a.obj P).val (η.app Q) hηQ
914	  have hi1 : IsIso (a.map (η.app P ▷ Q)) :=
915	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW1
916	  have hi2 : IsIso (a.map ((a.obj P).val ◁ η.app Q)) :=
917	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW2
918	  exact (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi1) ≪≫ (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi2)

14	`π_T : C ×_S T → T` of a relative curve, and the set-valued relative Picard

17	```
18	Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)

54	subtype. Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof"
55	we encode `OnProduct` by a typed `sorry` at the type level: the iter-175+ body
56	will instantiate it as a structure pairing a `(pullback πC πT).Modules` carrier
57	with an `IsInvertible` witness once that predicate is in Mathlib (or proven
58	internally as the project-side definition).

88	/-! ## §1. Line bundles on a product
89	
90	A *line bundle* on the relative curve `C ×_S T` is an invertible
91	`O_(C ×_S T)`-module, i.e. a sheaf of modules locally free of rank one. In
92	Mathlib `b80f227` the `Scheme.Modules` category is in place
93	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`), but there is no `IsInvertible`
94	predicate on its objects yet (the closest is `Module.Invertible R M` for rings,
95	in `Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`). iter-187 supplies the missing
96	predicate as a project-side definition via local trivialisation on an affine
97	open cover (see `IsLocallyTrivial` below), and refines `OnProduct` to the
98	corresponding subtype.
99	
100	Blueprint reference: `def:line_bundle_on_product` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
101	functor"; Stacks tag 01HK). -/
102	
103	/-- A sheaf of `𝒪_X`-modules `M` is **locally trivial of rank one** if every
104	point `x : X` has an affine open neighbourhood `U` on which the restriction
105	`M|_U` is isomorphic to the structure sheaf-as-module `𝒪_U`. This is the
106	project-side stand-in for the missing Mathlib `IsInvertible` predicate on
107	`Scheme.Modules`: line bundles (invertible sheaves) are exactly the
108	`IsLocallyTrivial` modules, by Stacks tag 01HK / Hartshorne II §6. Local
109	trivialisability is the structural content that survives base change along
110	`f : Y ⟶ X` (Stacks 01HH) and is the source of the invertibility-preservation
111	step for `pullbackAlongProjection`.
112	
113	The witness is bundled as `Nonempty (M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit _)`:
114	the existence of a sheaf-of-modules isomorphism over the affine chart. -/
115	def IsLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
116	  ∀ x : X, ∃ U : X.Opens, x ∈ U ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
117	    Nonempty (M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf)
118	
119	/-- The type of **line bundles on the product** `C ×_S T`: invertible sheaves of
120	`O_(C ×_S T)`-modules, where the product is the categorical pullback of the
121	projections `πC : C ⟶ S` and `πT : T ⟶ S`. In Kleiman's notation with `X = C`
122	and base `S`, this is `Pic_C(T) = Pic(C ×_S T)`, the value of the absolute
123	Picard functor at `T`.
124	
125	iter-187: refined to the subtype of `(Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules` cut out
126	by the project-side predicate `IsLocallyTrivial` (locally trivial of rank one).
127	The carrier is now genuinely the line-bundle subobject of `Scheme.Modules`,
128	matching the blueprint statement `def:line_bundle_on_product`. Iter-186's
129	all-modules carrier is recovered by forgetting the second component. -/
130	def OnProduct {S C T : Scheme.{u}} (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) : Type (u+1) :=
131	  { M : (Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules // IsLocallyTrivial M }
132	
133	/-- Projection from a line bundle on the product to its underlying
134	sheaf of modules. -/
135	abbrev OnProduct.carrier {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
136	    (L : OnProduct πC πT) : (Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules := L.1
137	
138	/-- A line bundle on the product is locally trivial of rank one. -/
139	lemma OnProduct.isLocallyTrivial {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
140	    (L : OnProduct πC πT) : IsLocallyTrivial L.carrier := L.2
141	
142	/-- **Stacks 01HH (pullback of an invertible sheaf is invertible).** For any
143	morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ X` and any locally-trivial sheaf of `𝒪_X`-modules
144	`M`, the pulled-back module `f^* M : Y.Modules` is again locally trivial.
145	
146	The proof unfolds an affine open cover trivialising `M`, pulls back along `f`,
147	and observes that `f^*` commutes with restriction along open immersions (the
148	`pullback`/`restrictFunctor` natural isomorphism in
149	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) so that the trivialising chart for
150	`M|_U` becomes a trivialising chart for `(f^* M)|_(f⁻¹ U)`. The full chart-chase
151	involves the natural-isomorphism `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` plus the
152	preservation of `SheafOfModules.unit` under pullback (Mathlib
153	`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackFree`'s
154	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit`). Iter-187 wraps this chase as a named typed sorry
155	(helper-budget = 1); iter-188+ instantiates the chart-chase. -/
156	lemma IsLocallyTrivial.pullback {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) {M : X.Modules}
157	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
158	    IsLocallyTrivial ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M) := by
159	  intro y
160	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, eM⟩ := hM (f.base y)
161	  have hyU' : y ∈ f ⁻¹ᵁ U := hxU
162	  obtain ⟨V, hV_aff, hyV, hVU⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset hyU'
163	  refine ⟨V, hyV, hV_aff, ?_⟩
164	  -- Stacks 01HH chart-chase: factor `V.ι ≫ f` through `U` using
165	  -- `Scheme.Hom.resLE`, then transport through `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`
166	  -- and `pullbackComp` to identify the pullback at `V` with the pullback of
167	  -- the unit on `U` to `V`. Close via `eM` and `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`
168	  -- (which is an iso since `Opens.map g.base` is final via the
169	  -- `RepresentablyFlat` instance).
170	  obtain ⟨eM⟩ := eM
171	  set g : (V : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := f.resLE U V hVU with hg_def
172	  have hg_comp : g ≫ U.ι = V.ι ≫ f := Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι f hVU
173	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final :=
174	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
175	  refine ⟨?_⟩
176	  -- 1: ((pullback f).obj M).restrict V.ι ≅ (pullback V.ι).obj ((pullback f).obj M)
177	  let i1 :=
178	    (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback V.ι).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M)
179	  -- 2: ≅ (pullback (V.ι ≫ f)).obj M
180	  let i2 := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp V.ι f).app M
181	  -- 3: ≅ (pullback (g ≫ U.ι)).obj M  (using V.ι ≫ f = g ≫ U.ι)
182	  let i3 := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hg_comp.symm).app M
183	  -- 4: ≅ (pullback g).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
184	  let i4 := ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g U.ι).symm).app M
185	  -- 5: ≅ (pullback g).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
186	  let i5 := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso
187	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M)
188	  -- 6: ≅ (pullback g).obj (unit_U)
189	  let i6 := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso eM
190	  -- 7: ≅ unit_V  (pullback of unit is unit via the Mathlib morphism, which is
191	  -- iso since the underlying `Opens.map g.base` is Final)
192	  let i7 := asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom)
193	  exact i1 ≪≫ i2 ≪≫ i3 ≪≫ i4 ≪≫ i5 ≪≫ i6 ≪≫ i7
194	
195	/-! ## §2. The pullback functor along the projection
196	
197	For a morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ Z` and an invertible `O_Z`-module `M`, the
198	pullback `f^* M` is an invertible `O_Y`-module (the pullback of an
199	open-cover-trivialisation of `M` trivialises `f^* M`, and the pullback of a free
200	module of rank one is free of rank one — Stacks tag 01HH). Specialising to the
201	projection `π_T = pullback.snd πC πT : C ×_S T ⟶ T` gives the pullback map
202	`π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)`. Mathlib's underlying `Scheme.Modules.pullback`
203	functor (in `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) supplies the pullback on
204	the full category of sheaves of modules; iter-175+ will restrict it to
205	invertible sheaves once the `OnProduct` carrier is unpacked.
206	
207	Blueprint reference: `def:pullback_along_projection` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag
208	01HH). -/
209	
210	/-- **Pullback along the projection** `π_T : C ×_S T ⟶ T` of a relative
211	curve. Sends a *line bundle on `T`* — i.e. an `O_T`-module `N` equipped with a
212	proof `hN : IsLocallyTrivial N` — to the line bundle `π_T^* N` on `C ×_S T`,
213	induced by the `Scheme.Modules.pullback` functor on `Scheme.Modules` together
214	with the preservation step `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` (Stacks 01HH).
215	
216	iter-187: refined to consume the `OnProduct`-style subtype on the source: the
217	underlying carrier is the direct application of Mathlib's
218	`Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)` functor at `N`, and the
219	locally-trivial witness comes from the project-side helper
220	`IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` (Stacks 01HH, named typed sorry pending the affine
221	chart chase). -/
222	noncomputable def pullbackAlongProjection {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
223	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (N : T.Modules) (hN : IsLocallyTrivial N) :
224	    OnProduct πC πT :=
225	  ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N,
226	    hN.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)⟩

228	/-! ## §3. Composition of pullbacks
229	
230	The composition law for line-bundle pullback (Stacks tag 01HG) says that for any
231	composable morphisms `f : X ⟶ Y, g : Y ⟶ Z` and any line bundle `M ∈ Pic(Z)`,
232	the natural isomorphism `(g ∘ f)^* M ≅ f^* g^* M` makes the pullback functor
233	into a (pseudo-)functor `Sch^op ⥤ Cat`.
234	
235	Specialised to the relative curve setting: given `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, the
236	base-change morphism `g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T` satisfies
237	`π_T ∘ g_C = g ∘ π_{T'}` (definition of the fiber product). Therefore on
238	line bundles `g_C^* ∘ π_T^* = π_{T'}^* ∘ g^*`, with the equality realised as the
239	canonical natural isomorphism of pullback functors via the pseudo-functoriality
240	of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` (Mathlib `Modules.pullbackComp`).
241	
242	Blueprint reference: `lem:pullback_compose` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag 01HG). -/
243	
244	/-- **Composition of line-bundle pullbacks** along the projection. For a base
245	scheme `S`, a curve-side morphism `πC : C ⟶ S`, test objects `T, T'` over `S`
246	via `πT : T ⟶ S` and `πT' : T' ⟶ S`, and a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`
247	(encoded by the hypothesis `πT' = g ≫ πT`), set
248	`g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T`
249	(the base-change morphism, given by `pullback.map`). On the underlying
250	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` functors the two routes through the canonical
251	naturality square give canonically isomorphic objects:
252	```
253	            Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)
254	  T.Modules  ─────────────────────────────────────────────►  (C ×_S T).Modules
255	       │                                                              │
256	       │ Scheme.Modules.pullback g            Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C
257	       ▼                                                              ▼
258	 T'.Modules  ─────────────────────────────────────────────►  (C ×_S T').Modules
259	            Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT')
260	```
261	i.e. for any `N : T.Modules`,
262	```
263	(Modules.pullback g_C).obj ((Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N)
264	  ≅
265	(Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT')).obj ((Modules.pullback g).obj N).
266	```
267	
268	This natural isomorphism is the substantive content of the
269	"line-bundle pullback functor is well-behaved under composition" fact
270	(Stacks tag 01HG). At the level of the `OnProduct` carrier (which is a typed
271	`sorry` in iter-174; see §1) it descends to equality of isomorphism classes
272	`[g_C^* π_T^* N] = [π_{T'}^* g^* N] ∈ OnProduct πC πT'`.
273	
274	iter-186: closed via the chain
275	1. `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g_C (pullback.snd πC πT)` (functor iso applied at `N`)
276	2. `Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr` for the equation `g_C ≫ snd πC πT = snd πC πT' ≫ g`
277	   (`pullback.lift_snd` on the `pullback.map` lift).
278	3. `(Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (pullback.snd πC πT') g).symm` (applied at `N`). -/
279	theorem pullback_pullback_eq {S C T T' : Scheme.{u}}
280	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T)
281	    (hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) (N : T.Modules) :
282	    Nonempty
283	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
284	            (Limits.pullback.map πC πT' πC πT (𝟙 C) g (𝟙 S)
285	              (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]) (by rw [Category.comp_id, hg]))).obj
286	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N) ≅
287	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT')).obj
288	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N)) := by
289	  set g_C : Limits.pullback πC πT' ⟶ Limits.pullback πC πT :=
290	    Limits.pullback.map πC πT' πC πT (𝟙 C) g (𝟙 S)
291	      (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]) (by rw [Category.comp_id, hg])
292	  have key : g_C ≫ Limits.pullback.snd πC πT = Limits.pullback.snd πC πT' ≫ g :=
293	    Limits.pullback.lift_snd _ _ _
294	  refine ⟨?_⟩
295	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g_C (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).app N ≪≫ ?_
296	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr key).app N ≪≫ ?_
297	  exact ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT') g).app N).symm
298	
299	end LineBundle

301	namespace RelPicPresheaf

303	/-! ## §4. The relative Picard quotient (set-valued)
304	
305	The relative Picard presheaf `Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`
306	quotients the line-bundle group of the product by the subgroup pulled back from
307	the base. As a quotient of abelian groups it is a quotient set; equivalently,
308	the equivalence relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` is captured by a
309	`Setoid` on the line-bundle type `OnProduct πC πT`. The quotient
310	`Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the underlying set of the relative
311	Picard presheaf at `T`.
312	
313	The "preimage_subgroup" name follows the Kleiman/Stacks convention: the
314	quotient is by the preimage `π_T^* Pic(T) ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T)`, treated as a
315	subgroup (image of a group homomorphism); the project-side Lean encoding
316	extracts the *Setoid* that the quotient construction takes.
317	
318	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined` (Kleiman §2,
319	Def. `df:Pfs`). -/
320	
321	/-- **Well-definedness of the relative Picard quotient as a `Setoid`.**
322	
323	The equivalence relation on `OnProduct πC πT` defining the relative Picard
324	presheaf
325	```
326	Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
327	```
328	is the relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`. This relation is reflexive
329	(`L ⊗ L⁻¹ = O_(C ×_S T) = π_T^* O_T ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`), symmetric (the inverse of
330	a line bundle in `π_T^* Pic(T)` is in `π_T^* Pic(T)`), and transitive
331	(`L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` and `L' ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` give
332	`L ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`); hence is an equivalence relation, encoded by a
333	`Setoid` on `OnProduct πC πT`.
334	
335	The substantive content is the well-definedness of the quotient: the canonical
336	projection `OnProduct πC πT → Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the
337	set-theoretic underlying map of the relative Picard presheaf at `T`.
338	
339	iter-186: with `OnProduct := (pullback πC πT).Modules`, the carrier is the
340	modules category. We encode the **iso-class setoid** `L ~ L' ↔ Nonempty (L ≅ L')`.
341	This is a substantive simplification: it identifies isomorphic modules
342	(giving the underlying set of `Pic(C ×_S T)` once invertibility is imposed),
343	but does not yet quotient by the pullback subgroup `π_T^* Pic(T)`. The full
344	quotient requires a tensor-product structure on `Scheme.Modules` (Mathlib
345	ships `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` but no monoidal structure on
346	`Scheme.Modules` directly at `b80f227`) plus an inverse for invertibles;
347	iter-187+: refine to `L ~ L' ↔ ∃ N : T.Modules, Nonempty (L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ≅
348	pullbackAlongProjection πC πT N)` once both are available. -/
349	noncomputable def preimage_subgroup {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
350	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) :
351	    Setoid (LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT) where
352	  r L L' := Nonempty (L.carrier ≅ L'.carrier)
353	  iseqv :=
354	    { refl := fun _ => ⟨Iso.refl _⟩
355	      symm := fun ⟨e⟩ => ⟨e.symm⟩
356	      trans := fun ⟨e₁⟩ ⟨e₂⟩ => ⟨e₁ ≪≫ e₂⟩ }
357	
358	/-! ## §5. Naturality in the test scheme — the relative Picard presheaf as a functor
359	
360	For a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S` (encoded by the equality `πT' = g ≫ πT`),
361	the base-change `g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T` induces a map
362	`g_C^* : Pic(C ×_S T) → Pic(C ×_S T')` on line bundles, which (by
363	`lem:pullback_compose` = `pullback_pullback_eq` above) sends the subgroup
364	`π_T^* Pic(T)` into `π_{T'}^* Pic(T')`. Therefore it descends to a unique map
365	on quotients
366	```
367	g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/S}(T'),    [L] ↦ [g_C^* L],
368	```
369	making `Pic^♯_{C/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` a presheaf.
370	
371	For the iter-174 file-skeleton, we pin the **object-level** functorial action
372	(i.e. the induced map of quotient sets for a single test morphism `g`). The
373	full `Functor` packaging (with the identity / composition laws supplied as
374	`map_id`, `map_comp` fields) is iter-175+ work once `OnProduct` and the
375	`preimage_subgroup` carrier are unpacked.
376	
377	Blueprint reference: `thm:pullback_natural` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
378	functor" + Def. `df:Pfs`; Stacks tag 01HG). -/
379	
380	/-- **The relative Picard presheaf is functorial in the test scheme.**
381	
382	Object-level functorial action: for any morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, there
383	is a canonical map
384	```
385	g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/S}(T')
386	```
387	of quotient sets, factoring `g_C^*` through `preimage_subgroup πC πT` and
388	`preimage_subgroup πC πT'`. On representatives, `g^♯ [L] = [g_C^* L]` where
389	`g_C := id_C ×_S g`. The factorisation through the quotient is well-defined
390	because `g_C^*` sends `π_T^* Pic(T) ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T)` into
391	`π_{T'}^* Pic(T') ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T')` (this is the commutative-square content of
392	`pullback_pullback_eq`).
393	
394	iter-186: with the iso-class setoid `preimage_subgroup`, `Quotient.lift`
395	on `L ↦ ⟦(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).obj L⟧` is well-defined because
396	functors preserve isomorphisms. The identity / composition laws giving the
397	full `Functor (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` packaging follow from `pullbackId` /
398	`pullbackComp` and are iter-187+ work (they currently aren't needed by any
399	downstream consumer). -/
400	noncomputable def functorial {S C T T' : Scheme.{u}}
401	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T) (hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) :
402	    Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT) → Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT') :=
403	  let g_C : Limits.pullback πC πT' ⟶ Limits.pullback πC πT :=
404	    Limits.pullback.map πC πT' πC πT (𝟙 C) g (𝟙 S)
405	      (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]) (by rw [Category.comp_id, hg])
406	  Quotient.lift
407	    (fun L : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT =>
408	      Quotient.mk (preimage_subgroup πC πT')
409	        (⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).obj L.carrier,
410	          L.isLocallyTrivial.pullback g_C⟩ : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT'))
411	    (fun _ _ ⟨e⟩ => Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).mapIso e⟩)
412	
413	end RelPicPresheaf
414	
415	end Scheme
416	
417	end AlgebraicGeometry

525	/-- **Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial.**
526	
527	If `M, N : X.Modules` are locally trivial of rank one (line bundles), so is
528	their tensor product `tensorObj M N`. Per blueprint
529	`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`. The proof picks, for each point `x`,
530	a common affine open `W ∋ x` contained in trivialising opens `U` (for `M`) and
531	`U'` (for `N`), refines both trivialisations to `W` via `restrictIsoUnitOfLE`,
532	then transports through `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, the bifunctoriality
533	`tensorObjIsoOfIso`, and the unit isomorphism `tensorObj_unit_iso`:
534	`(M ⊗ N)|_W ≅ M|_W ⊗ N|_W ≅ 𝒪_W ⊗ 𝒪_W ≅ 𝒪_W`. The only residual gap is the
535	substrate-restriction compatibility `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
536	lemma tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
537	    (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
538	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) := by
539	  intro x
540	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨eM⟩⟩ := hM x
541	  obtain ⟨U', hxU', hU'_aff, ⟨eN⟩⟩ := hN x
542	  obtain ⟨W, hW_aff, hxW, hWsub⟩ :=
543	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := x) (U := U ⊓ U') ⟨hxU, hxU'⟩
544	  have hWU : W ≤ U := le_trans hWsub inf_le_left
545	  have hWU' : W ≤ U' := le_trans hWsub inf_le_right
546	  refine ⟨W, hxW, hW_aff, ⟨?_⟩⟩
547	  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N ≪≫
548	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU eM) (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU' eN) ≪≫
549	    tensorObj_unit_iso

557	in `Ab`, whose forgetful functor reflects isos and preserves limits / filtered
558	colimits) together with `Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso` (restriction along an
559	open immersion commutes with stalks). **No stalk-⊗ ("d.2") is invoked**: this is a
560	statement about a single module morphism, never about the tensor stalk. -/

562	/-- **B-connector: a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on
563	an open cover is an isomorphism.** For `φ : M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules`, if every point
564	`x` lies in an open `U x` on which the restriction `(restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ`
565	is an isomorphism, then `φ` is an isomorphism. This is the B-bridge of the d.2-free
566	descent re-route assembling `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/

568	    (U : X → X.Opens) (hxU : ∀ x, x ∈ U x)

572	  have hst : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map
573	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
574	    intro x
575	    obtain ⟨x', hx'⟩ : ∃ x', (U x).ι x' = x := by
576	      have hmem : x ∈ (U x).ι.opensRange := by
577	        rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hxU x
578	      exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.mem_opensRange.mp hmem
579	    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ) := h x
580	    -- `(restrictFunctor … ⋙ toPresheaf … ⋙ stalkFunctor x').map φ` is iso (functor of an iso).
581	    haveI hFφ : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι ⋙
582	        Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x').map φ) := by
583	      dsimp only [Functor.comp_map]; exact Functor.map_isIso _ _
584	    -- Transport the iso across `restrictStalkNatIso` to the stalk at `(U x).ι x' = x`.
585	    have hGφ : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} ((U x).ι x')).map
586	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) :=
587	      (CategoryTheory.NatIso.isIso_map_iff
588	        (Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso (U x).ι x') φ).mp hFφ
589	    exact hx' ▸ hGφ
590	  -- Package as a morphism of `TopCat.Sheaf Ab X` and apply the stalkwise iso criterion.
591	  let MS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩
592	  let NS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨N.presheaf, N.isSheaf⟩
593	  let fS : MS ⟶ NS := ⟨(Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ⟩
594	  haveI : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map fS.hom) := hst
595	  haveI hSiso : IsIso fS := TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso fS
596	  have h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ) := by
597	    have := (TopCat.Sheaf.forget Ab.{u} X).map_isIso fS
598	    exact this

602	morphism.** Given a morphism `g : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf` of the underlying
603	`Ab`-presheaves that is sectionwise `𝒪_X`-linear, package it as a morphism `M ⟶ N`
604	of `𝒪_X`-modules. This wraps `PresheafOfModules.homMk` at the `Scheme.Modules` level;
605	it is the "promote to `𝒪_X`-linear" half of the descent A-bridge `homOfLocalCompat`

607	Sectionwise linearity is a property the consumer checks on a separated presheaf. -/
608	noncomputable def homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
609	    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
610	    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
611	      (g.app V).hom (r • m) = r • (g.app V).hom m) :
612	    M ⟶ N :=
613	  ⟨PresheafOfModules.homMk (M₁ := M.val) (M₂ := N.val) g hg⟩
614	
615	/-- The underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism of `homMk g hg` is `g` itself. -/
616	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map_homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
617	    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
618	    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
619	      (g.app V).hom (r • m) = r • (g.app V).hom m) :
620	    (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map (homMk g hg) = g :=
621	  rfl

51:section mateEquiv
82:def mateEquiv : TwoSquare G L₁ L₂ H ≃ TwoSquare R₁ H G R₂ where
120:theorem mateEquiv_counit (α : TwoSquare G L₁ L₂ H) (d : D) :
121:    L₂.map ((mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app _) ≫ adj₂.counit.app _ =
125:theorem mateEquiv_counit_symm (α : TwoSquare R₁ H G R₂) (d : D) :
127:      ((mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α).app _ ≫ H.map (adj₁.counit.app d) := by
128:  conv_lhs => rw [← (mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).right_inv α]
129:  exact (mateEquiv_counit adj₁ adj₂ ((mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α) d)
133:theorem unit_mateEquiv (α : TwoSquare G L₁ L₂ H) (c : C) :
134:    G.map (adj₁.unit.app c) ≫ (mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app _ =
136:  simp only [id_obj, comp_obj, mateEquiv, Equiv.coe_fn_mk, comp_app, rightUnitor_inv_app,
148:theorem unit_mateEquiv_symm (α : TwoSquare R₁ H G R₂) (c : C) :
150:      adj₂.unit.app _ ≫ R₂.map (((mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α).app _) := by
151:  conv_lhs => rw [← (mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).right_inv α]
152:  exact (unit_mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ ((mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α) c)
154:end mateEquiv
156:section mateEquivVComp
167:theorem mateEquiv_vcomp (α : TwoSquare G₁ L₁ L₂ H₁) (β : TwoSquare G₂ L₂ L₃ H₂) :
168:    (mateEquiv adj₁ adj₃) (α ≫ₕ β) = (mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α) ≫ᵥ (mateEquiv adj₂ adj₃ β) := by
169:  unfold hComp vComp mateEquiv
192:end mateEquivVComp
194:section mateEquivHComp
206:theorem mateEquiv_hcomp (α : TwoSquare G L₁ L₂ H) (β : TwoSquare H L₃ L₄ K) :
207:    (mateEquiv (adj₁.comp adj₃) (adj₂.comp adj₄)) (α ≫ᵥ β) =
208:      (mateEquiv adj₃ adj₄ β) ≫ₕ (mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α) := by
209:  unfold vComp hComp mateEquiv Adjunction.comp
223:end mateEquivHComp
225:section mateEquivSquareComp
242:theorem mateEquiv_square (α : TwoSquare G₁ L₁ L₃ H₁) (β : TwoSquare H₁ L₂ L₄ K₁)
244:    (mateEquiv (adj₁.comp adj₂) (adj₅.comp adj₆)) ((α ≫ᵥ β) ≫ₕ (γ ≫ᵥ δ)) =
245:      ((mateEquiv adj₂ adj₄ β) ≫ₕ (mateEquiv adj₁ adj₃ α))
246:         ≫ᵥ ((mateEquiv adj₄ adj₆ δ) ≫ₕ (mateEquiv adj₃ adj₅ γ)) := by
248:    mateEquiv_vcomp (adj₁.comp adj₂) (adj₃.comp adj₄) (adj₅.comp adj₆) (α ≫ᵥ β) (γ ≫ᵥ δ)
249:  simp only [mateEquiv_hcomp] at vcomp
252:end mateEquivSquareComp
254:section conjugateEquiv
263:defined as a special case of `mateEquiv`, where the two "vertical" functors are identity, modulo
264:composition with the unitors. Corresponding natural transformations are called `conjugateEquiv`.
269:This is in contrast to the general case `mateEquiv` which does not in general have this property.
272:def conjugateEquiv : (L₂ ⟶ L₁) ≃ (R₁ ⟶ R₂) :=
276:    _ ≃ _ := mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂
281:theorem conjugateEquiv_counit (α : L₂ ⟶ L₁) (d : D) :
282:    L₂.map ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app _) ≫ adj₂.counit.app d =
287:theorem conjugateEquiv_counit_symm (α : R₁ ⟶ R₂) (d : D) :
289:      ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α).app _ ≫ adj₁.counit.app d := by
290:    conv_lhs => rw [← (conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).right_inv α]
291:    exact (conjugateEquiv_counit adj₁ adj₂ ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α) d)
294:theorem unit_conjugateEquiv (α : L₂ ⟶ L₁) (c : C) :
295:    adj₁.unit.app _ ≫ (conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app _ =
297:  dsimp [conjugateEquiv]

279	
280	/-- A component of a transposed form of the conjugation definition. -/
281	theorem conjugateEquiv_counit (α : L₂ ⟶ L₁) (d : D) :
282	    L₂.map ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app _) ≫ adj₂.counit.app d =
283	      α.app _ ≫ adj₁.counit.app d := by
284	  simp
285	
286	/-- A component of a transposed form of the inverse conjugation definition. -/
287	theorem conjugateEquiv_counit_symm (α : R₁ ⟶ R₂) (d : D) :
288	    L₂.map (α.app _) ≫ adj₂.counit.app d =
289	      ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α).app _ ≫ adj₁.counit.app d := by
290	    conv_lhs => rw [← (conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).right_inv α]
291	    exact (conjugateEquiv_counit adj₁ adj₂ ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α) d)

294	theorem unit_conjugateEquiv (α : L₂ ⟶ L₁) (c : C) :

296	      adj₂.unit.app c ≫ R₂.map (α.app _) := by

306	    adj₁.unit.app _ ≫ α.app _ =
307	      adj₂.unit.app c ≫ R₂.map (((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α).app _) := by
308	    conv_lhs => rw [← (conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).right_inv α]
309	    exact (unit_conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α) c)

  rw [SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit]
  sorry

863	/-! ## §6. Pullback-monoidality substrate (A.1.c): `IsInvertible.pullback`
864	
865	Project-local Mathlib supplement. The relative Picard consumer re-bases onto the
866	`IsInvertible` carrier and its structure maps are *pullback* maps for GENERAL scheme
867	morphisms (the projection `C ×_S T → T` and base-change maps are neither open
868	immersions nor flat). We need that pullback preserves `⊗`-invertibility. This requires
869	`pullbackTensorIso` (`f^*(M ⊗ N) ≅ f^*M ⊗ f^*N`) and `pullbackUnitIso`
870	(`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`). Blueprint §`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`. -/
871

/-- **Sheafification reconciles the presheaf tensor with the tensor of the
input.new_string:
`pullbackTensorIso` (`f^*(M ⊗ N) ≅ f^*M ⊗ f^*N`) and `pullbackUnitIso`
(`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`). Blueprint §`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`. -/

/-- **Composition coherence of the unit→pushforward-unit comparison.**

For composable scheme morphisms `h : Z ⟶ Y`, `f : Y ⟶ X`, the canonical comparison
`unitToPushforwardObjUnit` of the composite `h ≫ f` factors through the comparisons
of `f` and `h` and the pushforward pseudofunctor coherence `pushforwardComp`. This is
the *pushforward-side* (right-adjoint) half of the composition coherence; it is
concrete (sectionwise it is just functoriality of the structure-sheaf ring maps,
hence `rfl` after the `ext`-chain) and is the pushforward-side input from which the
genuinely-needed *pullback-side* coherence of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is obtained by
adjunction-mate transport. Mathlib-absent at the pinned commit. -/
lemma unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z ⟶ Y) (f : Y ⟶ X) :
    SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom =
      SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
          (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf) := by
  apply SheafOfModules.Hom.ext
  apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext
  intro U
  apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
  apply LinearMap.ext
  intro a
  rfl

import Mathlib
open CategoryTheory AlgebraicGeometry

universe u

lemma uTPf_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z ⟶ Y) (f : Y ⟶ X) :
    SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom =
      SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
          (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf) := by
  apply SheafOfModules.Hom.ext
  apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext
  intro U
  apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
  apply LinearMap.ext
  intro a
  rfl

example {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z ⟶ Y) (f : Y ⟶ X) :
    SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom =
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv.app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
      SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
  have key := uTPf_comp h f
  have conj := unit_conjugateEquiv
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h))
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f))
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv] at conj
  apply (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f)).homEquiv _ _ |>.injective
  have hL := SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
    (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom
  rw [hL, key, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit, ← conj]
  sorry

import Mathlib
open CategoryTheory AlgebraicGeometry

universe u

lemma uTPf_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z ⟶ Y) (f : Y ⟶ X) :
    SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom =
      SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
          (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf) := by
  apply SheafOfModules.Hom.ext
  apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext
  intro U
  apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
  apply LinearMap.ext
  intro a
  rfl

example {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z ⟶ Y) (f : Y ⟶ X) :
    SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom =
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv.app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
      SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
  have key := uTPf_comp h f
  have conj := unit_conjugateEquiv
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h))
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f))
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv] at conj
  apply (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f)).homEquiv _ _ |>.injective
  have hL : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f)).homEquiv _ _
      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom)
    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom := by
    exact SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
      (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom
  have hi : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f)).homEquiv _ _
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv.app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))
    = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h)).unit.app
          (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.app
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pullback h).obj
            (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) := by
    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]; exact conj.symm
  have hLf : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _
      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)
    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom := by
    exact SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
      f.toRingCatSheafHom
  have hLh : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h).homEquiv _ _
      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
    exact SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
      h.toRingCatSheafHom
  have hinner : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h).unit.app
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) ≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward h).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
          SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
    = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
        SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
    have e := Adjunction.homEquiv_unit (adj := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h)
      (X := (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))
      (Y := SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf)
      (f := (Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
          (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
    have key2 : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h).homEquiv _ _
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
              (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
            SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
        = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
            SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
      rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left]; exact congrArg _ hLh
    exact e.symm.trans key2
  have hcomp' : ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h)).unit.app
        (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward h ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
          SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
          (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom) := by
    have ef := Adjunction.homEquiv_unit (adj := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)
      (X := SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (Y := SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf)
      (f := SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)
    rw [Adjunction.comp_unit_app, Functor.comp_map]
    erw [Category.assoc]
    rw [← Functor.map_comp, hinner, Functor.map_comp]
    erw [← Category.assoc]
    rw [show (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).unit.app
            (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)
        = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom from ef.symm.trans hLf]
  rw [hL, key, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right, hi]
  erw [Category.assoc]
  rw [← (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.naturality
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)]
  erw [← Category.assoc]
  rw [hcomp']
  erw [Category.assoc]

/-- **Composition coherence of the unit comparison `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`
(the genuinely-new ingredient for `pullbackUnitIso`).**

For composable scheme morphisms `h : Z ⟶ Y`, `f : Y ⟶ X`, the canonical comparison
`f^*𝒪 ⟶ 𝒪` of the composite `h ≫ f` factors through the comparisons of `f` and `h`
and the pullback pseudofunctor coherence `pullbackComp`:
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h≫f) = (pullbackComp h f).inv ≫ (pullback h).map (pbu f) ≫ pbu h`.

This is the pullback-side (left-adjoint) composition coherence — Mathlib-absent at the
pinned commit and NOT a sectionwise statement (the abstract left-adjoint pullback has no
sectionwise value). It is obtained by adjunction-mate transport from the pushforward-side
coherence `unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp`: transposing both sides under
`pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h≫f)`, the left side becomes `unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h≫f)`
and the right side is reassembled via the conjugate/mate identity
`conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` (relating `pullbackComp.inv` to `pushforwardComp.hom`),
`unit_conjugateEquiv`, and the composite-adjunction unit `Adjunction.comp_unit_app`.

Consumed by `pullbackUnitIso`: on an affine chart `V` the inclusion `V.ι ≫ f` factors as
`g ≫ U.ι` with `Opens.map g.base` `Final`, so two applications of this coherence (one for
each factorisation) express the restricted global comparison as a composite of isomorphisms
(`pbu` for an open immersion / a `Final`-chart morphism is an iso), whence
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an iso by `isIso_of_isIso_restrict`.

The proof uses `erw` for the associativity / functoriality steps because the `SheafOfModules`
category compositions appear in defeq-but-not-syntactic forms (`Scheme.Modules.pullback f`
vs `SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom`) on which plain `rw [Category.assoc]` /
`rw [Functor.map_comp]` fail to unify. -/
lemma pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z ⟶ Y) (f : Y ⟶ X) :
    SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom =
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv.app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
      SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
  have key := unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp h f
  have conj := unit_conjugateEquiv
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h))
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f))
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv] at conj
  apply (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f)).homEquiv _ _ |>.injective
  have hL : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f)).homEquiv _ _
      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom)
    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom := by
    exact SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
      (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom
  have hi : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f)).homEquiv _ _
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv.app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))
    = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h)).unit.app
          (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.app
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pullback h).obj
            (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) := by
    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]; exact conj.symm
  have hLf : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _
      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)
    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom := by
    exact SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
      f.toRingCatSheafHom
  have hLh : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h).homEquiv _ _
      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
    exact SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
      h.toRingCatSheafHom
  have hinner : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h).unit.app
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)) ≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward h).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
          SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
    = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
        SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
    have e := Adjunction.homEquiv_unit (adj := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h)
      (X := (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))
      (Y := SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf)
      (f := (Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
          (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
    have key2 : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h).homEquiv _ _
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
              (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
            SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
        = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
            SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
      rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left]; exact congrArg _ hLh
    exact e.symm.trans key2
  have hcomp' : ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h)).unit.app
        (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward h ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map
            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
          SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)
    = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
          (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom) := by
    have ef := Adjunction.homEquiv_unit (adj := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)
      (X := SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (Y := SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf)
      (f := SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)
    rw [Adjunction.comp_unit_app, Functor.comp_map]
    erw [Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, hinner, Functor.map_comp]
    erw [← Category.assoc]
    rw [show (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).unit.app
            (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)
        = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom from ef.symm.trans hLf]
    rfl
  rw [hL, key, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right, hi]
  erw [Category.assoc, ← (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.naturality
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom)]
  erw [← Category.assoc, hcomp']

input.new_string:
erw [← Category.assoc, hcomp']
  erw [Category.assoc]

/-- **On an affine-style chart `V ≤ f⁻¹ U`, the restriction of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit f`
is an isomorphism.** Per-chart half of `pullbackUnitIso`: factor `V.ι ≫ f = g ≫ U.ι`
through `g = f.resLE U V`, whose `Opens.map g.base` is `Final` (so `pbu g` is an iso),
and use the composition coherence `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` on both factorisations to
exhibit the restricted comparison as a composite of isomorphisms. -/
lemma isIso_restrict_pullbackObjUnitToUnit {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
    {V : Y.Opens} {U : X.Opens} (hVU : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor V.ι).map
      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)) := by
  set g := Scheme.Hom.resLE f U V hVU with hg_def
  have hg : g ≫ U.ι = V.ι ≫ f := Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι f hVU
  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map V.ι.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map U.ι.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  rw [NatIso.isIso_map_iff (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback V.ι)
    (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)]
  haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).map
      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit U.ι.toRingCatSheafHom)) := Functor.map_isIso _ _
  haveI hVιf : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (V.ι ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom) := by
    rw [← hg, pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp g U.ι]
    infer_instance
  haveI hD : IsIso
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp V.ι f).inv.app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).map
            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
          SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit V.ι.toRingCatSheafHom) :=
    (pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp V.ι f) ▸ hVιf
  have h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).map
        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
      SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit V.ι.toRingCatSheafHom) :=
    IsIso.of_isIso_comp_left _ _
  exact IsIso.of_isIso_comp_right _ _

/-- **Pullback preserves the structure sheaf (the unit isomorphism `f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`).**
Phase 1 of the pullback-monoidality substrate (blueprint `lem:pullback_unit_iso`). The
canonical Mathlib comparison `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an isomorphism for
a *general* scheme morphism `f`: it is checked locally on affine charts via the axiom-clean
`isIso_of_isIso_restrict`, each chart closed by `isIso_restrict_pullbackObjUnitToUnit`. -/
noncomputable def pullbackUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅
      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf := by
  have hchart : ∀ y : Y, ∃ (V : Y.Opens) (U : X.Opens), y ∈ V ∧ V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U := by
    intro y
    obtain ⟨W, _, hxW, -⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset
      (show f.base y ∈ (⊤ : X.Opens) from trivial)
    have hyW : y ∈ f ⁻¹ᵁ W := hxW
    obtain ⟨V, _, hyV, hVW⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset hyW
    exact ⟨V, W, hyV, hVW⟩
  choose Vf Uf hyV hVU using hchart
  haveI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) :=
    isIso_of_isIso_restrict _ Vf hyV
      (fun y => isIso_restrict_pullbackObjUnitToUnit f (hVU y))
  exact asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)

import Mathlib

-- pbu of an open-immersion .ι
example {X : Scheme.{u}} (U : X.Opens) :
    IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit U.ι.toRingCatSheafHom) := by
  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map U.ι.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  infer_instance
-- pbu of g where g : V ⟶ U  (resLE) -- recheck with explicit
example {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) {U : X.Opens} {V : Y.Opens} (hVU : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.resLE f U V hVU)).map
      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit U.ι.toRingCatSheafHom)) := by
  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map U.ι.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  haveI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit U.ι.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
  exact Functor.map_isIso _ _
-- pullbackComp.inv.app iso
example {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z ⟶ Y) (f : Y ⟶ X) (M : X.Modules) :
    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv.app M) := by infer_instance

1046	/-- **Pullback preserves the structure sheaf (the unit isomorphism `f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`).**

1048	canonical Mathlib comparison `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an isomorphism for

1050	`isIso_of_isIso_restrict`, each chart closed by `isIso_restrict_pullbackObjUnitToUnit`. -/
1051	noncomputable def pullbackUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) :
1052	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅
1053	      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf := by

1055	    intro y
1056	    obtain ⟨W, _, hxW, -⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset
1057	      (show f.base y ∈ (⊤ : X.Opens) from trivial)
1058	    have hyW : y ∈ f ⁻¹ᵁ W := hxW
1059	    obtain ⟨V, _, hyV, hVW⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset hyW
1060	    exact ⟨V, W, hyV, hVW⟩
1061	  choose Vf Uf hyV hVU using hchart

1063	    isIso_of_isIso_restrict _ Vf hyV
1064	      (fun y => isIso_restrict_pullbackObjUnitToUnit f (hVU y))

1066	
1067	/-- **Sheafification reconciles the presheaf tensor with the tensor of the
1068	sheafified factors.** For presheaves of modules `P, Q` on `X`, sheafifying the
1069	presheaf tensor `P ⊗ Q` agrees with sheafifying the tensor of the underlying
1070	presheaves of their sheafifications `(a P).val ⊗ (a Q).val`, where
1071	`a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 𝒪_X)`. This is the "sheafification is

/-- **On an affine-style chart `V ≤ f⁻¹ U`, the restriction of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit f`
is an isomorphism.** Per-chart half of `pullbackUnitIso`: factor `V.ι ≫ f = g ≫ U.ι`
through `g = f.resLE U V`, whose `Opens.map g.base` is `Final` (so `pbu g` is an iso),
and use the composition coherence `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` on both factorisations to
exhibit the restricted comparison as a composite of isomorphisms. -/
lemma isIso_restrict_pullbackObjUnitToUnit {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
    {V : Y.Opens} {U : X.Opens} (hVU : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor V.ι).map
      (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)) := by
  have hg : Scheme.Hom.resLE f U V hVU ≫ U.ι = V.ι ≫ f := Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι f hVU
  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map (Scheme.Hom.resLE f U V hVU).base).Final :=
    final_of_representablyFlat _
  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map V.ι.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map U.ι.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  haveI hVιf : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (V.ι ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom) := by
    rw [← hg, pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp (Scheme.Hom.resLE f U V hVU) U.ι]
    refine @IsIso.comp_isIso _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ?_ (@IsIso.comp_isIso _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ?_ ?_)
    · infer_instance
    · exact Functor.map_isIso _ _
    · infer_instance
  rw [NatIso.isIso_map_iff (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback V.ι)
    (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)]
  haveI hD : IsIso
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp V.ι f).inv.app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).map
            (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
          SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit V.ι.toRingCatSheafHom) :=
    (pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp V.ι f) ▸ hVιf
  have h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).map
        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
      SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit V.ι.toRingCatSheafHom) :=
    IsIso.of_isIso_comp_left _ _
  exact IsIso.of_isIso_comp_right _ _

/-- **Pullback preserves the structure sheaf (the unit isomorphism `f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`).**
Phase 1 of the pullback-monoidality substrate (blueprint `lem:pullback_unit_iso`). The
canonical Mathlib comparison `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an isomorphism for
a *general* scheme morphism `f`: it is checked locally on affine charts via the axiom-clean
`isIso_of_isIso_restrict`, each chart closed by `isIso_restrict_pullbackObjUnitToUnit`. -/
noncomputable def pullbackUnitIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅
      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf := by
  have hchart : ∀ y : Y, ∃ (V : Y.Opens) (U : X.Opens), y ∈ V ∧ V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U := by
    intro y
    obtain ⟨W, _, hxW, -⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset
      (show f.base y ∈ (⊤ : X.Opens) from trivial)
    have hyW : y ∈ f ⁻¹ᵁ W := hxW
    obtain ⟨V, _, hyV, hVW⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset hyW
    exact ⟨V, W, hyV, hVW⟩
  choose Vf Uf hyV hVU using hchart
  haveI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) :=
    isIso_of_isIso_restrict _ Vf hyV
      (fun y => isIso_restrict_pullbackObjUnitToUnit f (hVU y))
  exact asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom)

/- **HANDOFF — `pullbackUnitIso` (Phase 1, blueprint `lem:pullback_unit_iso`): the hard
linchpin `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` IS LANDED (above, axiom-clean); the chart-chase
assembly is fully worked out but BLOCKED on a Lean instance-synthesis quirk (NOT math).

The complete recipe (per-chart lemma `isIso_restrict_pullbackObjUnitToUnit` + globalizer):
  • For `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U`, set `g := f.resLE U V`; then `g ≫ U.ι = V.ι ≫ f` (`resLE_comp_ι`),
    and `Opens.map g.base`, `Opens.map V.ι.base`, `Opens.map U.ι.base` are all `Final`
    (`final_of_representablyFlat`), so `pbu g`, `pbu V.ι`, `pbu U.ι` are isos
    (`instIsIsoPullbackObjUnitToUnitOfFinal`).
  • `pbu (V.ι ≫ f)` is an iso: `rw [← hg, pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp g U.ι]` exhibits it as
    `(pullbackComp g U.ι).inv.app ≫ (pullback g).map (pbu U.ι) ≫ pbu g`, a composite of isos.
  • Transport across `restrictFunctorIsoPullback V.ι` (`NatIso.isIso_map_iff`): the goal
    becomes `IsIso ((pullback V.ι).map (pbu f))`.
  • `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp V.ι f` gives `pbu (V.ι≫f) = (pullbackComp V.ι f).inv.app ≫
    (pullback V.ι).map (pbu f) ≫ pbu V.ι`; transport `IsIso (pbu (V.ι≫f))` across it (`▸`),
    then `IsIso.of_isIso_comp_left`/`of_isIso_comp_right` cancel the two outer isos to give
    `IsIso ((pullback V.ι).map (pbu f))`.
  • `pullbackUnitIso`: pick (per point) `V ∋ y` with `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U` (two
    `exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset` calls), `choose`, feed to `isIso_of_isIso_restrict`,
    then `asIso (pbu f)`.

THE ONLY BLOCKER (verified this iter): inside the per-chart lemma's multi-hypothesis context,
`infer_instance` FAILS to synthesise `IsIso (pbu U.ι)`, `IsIso (pbu g)`, and even
`IsIso ((pullbackComp g U.ι).inv.app _)` — although ALL of these synthesise fine STANDALONE
(verified via `lean_run_code`). The `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ` term carries
non-canonical implicit instance arguments (`[(pushforward φ).IsRightAdjoint]`,
`[F.IsContinuous]`); after `rw [pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp …]` the produced `pbu`/`pullbackComp`
subterms bind those implicits to forms that the in-scope `(Opens.map _).Final` haveIs and the
`OfFinal` instance no longer match (and pre-established `haveI` copies match the head but fail
unification, blocking backtracking). `set`/`let`-binding `g` is NOT the cause (inlining
`f.resLE U V hVU` does not help). NEXT-ITER FIX (recommended, in order):
  (1) build each component iso as a fully type-ascribed named `Iso` BEFORE the
      `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` rewrite, then `convert` / transport the IsIso across the
      coherence equation, so the implicits are pinned by the ascription rather than re-synthesised;
  (2) or prove a tiny wrapper `IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ)` given
      `[(Opens.map φ-functor).Final]` as a `@[instance] lemma` with the canonical implicit
      shape, and use it explicitly (`@`-applied) at each site;
  (3) or `mathlib-analogist` consult on `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit` instance
      canonicity. The math/structure is COMPLETE — this is pure instance plumbing. -/

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added (2, both axiom-clean — `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`):**
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp` (~L880) — pushforward-side
    (right-adjoint) composition coherence of the unit comparison.
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` (~L930) — **THE genuinely-new
    ingredient** for Phase 1 (`lem:pullback_unit_iso`): the *pullback-side* composition coherence
    `pbu(h≫f) = (pullbackComp h f).inv ≫ (pullback h).map(pbu f) ≫ pbu h`, obtained by
    adjunction-mate transport. Mathlib-absent at the pinned commit; not a sectionwise statement.
- **Declarations blocked (2):** `isIso_restrict_pullbackObjUnitToUnit` + `pullbackUnitIso`
  (Phase 1 deliverable) — full recipe worked out and correct; blocked on a Lean
  instance-synthesis quirk (NOT math). Recipe + precise blocker recorded in a HANDOFF comment in

     factor by `hi` (= `homEquiv_unit` ∘ `conj`); collapse the composite-adjunction unit via two
